items.html: Afterwards, the Malchut was sawn apart [Nesirah], after her Partzuf had been corrected by means of ZA [Zeir Anpin] — for to that end she had been attached to him back to back: in order to receive her Mochin and her illuminations through him. At that time the illuminations would pass to her through the partitions of ZA, and therefore they were Dinim [judgments]; and that door and hinge and MaNTzePaCh that she received through him in her Yesod were Dinim, and they were called discernments of Jerusalem, which is Dinim. Afterwards she was sawn apart — that is, the NHY [Netzah-Hod-Yesod] of Ima and the Mochin departed from ZA and went out of his head, and the NHY of Ima, together with the Mochin, entered the head of the female standing at the back of ZA. Then her Lights and her Mochin are not drawn through ZA, but from Ima herself; and therefore she is sawn apart and separated from the back of ZA.
